Find the minimum value of the function y = e^x - x  
TO FIND THE MINIMUM VALUE OF THE FUNCTION Y = E^X - X WE TAKE THE DERIVATIVE OF THE FUNCTION WITH RESPECT TO X AND SET IT EQUAL TO ZERO THE DERIVATIVE OF Y WITH RESPECT TO X IS E^X - 1 SETTING THIS EQUAL TO ZERO GIVES E^X - 1 = 0 SOLVING FOR X WE GET E^X = 1 WHICH MEANS X = 0 TO DETERMINE IF THIS IS A MINIMUM WE TAKE THE SECOND DERIVATIVE OF THE FUNCTION THE SECOND DERIVATIVE IS E^X SINCE E^X IS ALWAYS POSITIVE THE FUNCTION IS CONCAVE UP AT X = 0 SO THIS IS A MINIMUM THE VALUE OF THE FUNCTION AT X = 0 IS E^0 - 0 = 1 - 0 = 1 THEREFORE THE MINIMUM VALUE OF THE FUNCTION IS 1
